subject: Databases
title: Running Your First Query
description: A fast breakdown of Running Your First Query for builders.

hook.text: SELECT 1 + 1; — run that in any SQL client and it returns 2. Your first query, no tables needed.

buildup.text: SELECT is the most common SQL command. It retrieves data. Every analyst and developer uses it daily.

discovery.text: SELECT * FROM users returns every column for every user. The star means all columns.

twist.text: Never use SELECT * in production. Fetching unused columns wastes bandwidth and slows the query.

climax.text: SELECT name, email FROM users — ask for exactly what you need. Nothing more, nothing less.

punchline.text: SELECT starts every read. Be specific about what you ask for.

quiz.question: Why should you avoid SELECT * in production?
options: It fetches all columns including ones you don't need, wasting resources
It deletes all data from the table
It only works in test environments
